original_draft.source_quote: No power at all — no LEDs, no rail activity after connecting wall or battery power. Likely cause: Blown input fuse F1 on the main power-input path, or no voltage reaching sense resistor R49. Probe R49 for input voltage with DMM; if absent, check F1 for continuity in diode-mode/resistance. Replace F1 if open (SMD fuse).

evidence.reasoning: F1 is named literally and is in the graph; bridge maps 'input fuse' alias to refdes F1.
Quote describes F1 as open (blown), justifying mode=open.

evidence.source_quote_substring: No power at all — no LEDs, no rail activity
evidence.reasoning: F1 is source_refdes of rail VIN per the graph; total loss of power matches VIN being offline, which cascades to all downstream rails.

evidence.source_quote_substring: no voltage reaching sense resistor R49
evidence.reasoning: Loss of input voltage at R49 implies VIN rail is dead; LPC_VCC (sourced by U14 from VIN) is consequently dead.

original_draft.reasoning_summary: Blown SMD input fuse F1 opens the VIN rail; per the graph F1 is the source_refdes for VIN. Without VIN, the always-on LPC_VCC regulator (U14) cannot produce its rail, so LPC_VCC dies as well, matching 'no LEDs, no rail activity'.

original_draft.source_quote: SD card boot fails; system won't read SD card even though LPC and 3.3 V main rail are confirmed live. Likely cause: 1.8 V rail dead — level shifters U28 and U8 require both 3.3 V and 1.8 V to operate, blocking the SD interface. Probe C130 for 1.8 V; if absent, identify 1.8 V buck regulator in schematic (Power section), check for short on output in diode-mode before reflowing or replacing.

Leaky MLCC modeled as ~200Ω partial short.
C130 is on 1.8V net (VCCIO per graph); leaky cap collapses the rail.

evidence.reasoning: Loss of 1.8V kills the SD level shifters; U28 in graph (U8 not present).

original_draft.reasoning_summary: Leaky C130 MLCC partially shorts the 1.8V (VCCIO) rail. Per graph, VCCIO has C130 as decoupling cap; with the rail collapsed, level shifter U28 can't operate and SD card boot fails.

original_draft.source_quote: Internal display flickers intermittently or goes blank after reassembly; HDMI output unaffected. Likely cause: Damaged or mis-seated J24 MIPI-DSI flex cable (30-pin, 0.5 mm pitch) between CPU module and SN65DSI86 eDP bridge. cold joint on SN65DSI86 BGA/QFN pin — reflow restores DSI link

evidence.reasoning: Alias 'SN65DSI86' / 'eDP bridge' for U10; bridge maps to U10.
Cold joint = open connection on signal pin → mode=open (local signal failure, not rail).

original_draft.source_quote: LPC UART responds and D11 is lit, but main CPU module does not start after sending `1P` power-on command; D11 goes out and nothing further happens. Likely cause: LPC GPIO lines to main power rail switchers damaged or a downstream rail is shorted, causing immediate shutdown.

evidence.reasoning: Alias 'LPC' for U18 (System Controller / LPC11U24); bridge maps to U18.

evidence.source_quote_substring: LPC GPIO lines to main power rail switchers damaged
evidence.reasoning: Damaged GPIO → MCU can't enable downstream rails; treat as dead.

evidence.source_quote_substring: main CPU module does not start
evidence.reasoning: U18 controls enables for switched rails (3V3_PWR_AUX, USB_PWR per graph topology). Damaged GPIO leaves these disabled.

original_draft.reasoning_summary: U18 LPC11U24 GPIO damage prevents enabling the downstream switched rails. Per the graph, 3V3_PWR_AUX (U27) and USB_PWR (U24) are the controller-gated supplies — their enables originate from U18, so a dead GPIO leaves them off.

original_draft.source_quote: No power at all — no LEDs, no rail activity after connecting wall or battery power. Likely cause: Blown input fuse F1 on the main power-input path, or no voltage reaching sense resistor R49. input sense resistor open or cold-joint, breaking VIN path

evidence.reasoning: R49 named literally; bridge maps to R49.

evidence.source_quote_substring: input sense resistor open or cold-joint
evidence.reasoning: Open R49 breaks VIN path → mode=open.
R49 sits in series on the input path feeding VIN; an open R49 starves all downstream rails sourced from VIN.

original_draft.reasoning_summary: Open R49 breaks the input current path; VIN cannot reach the downstream regulators. U14 (sourcing LPC_VCC from VIN) loses its input, so LPC_VCC also dies — matching 'no LEDs, no rail activity'.
